redFlags.evidence: The Bloch-Bauer estate was seized after the 1938 Anschluss; the painting passed to the Austrian Gallery Belvedere, which held it for close to six decades.
Title was disputed until an Austrian arbitration panel ruled for the heirs in 2006. The gap is documented, not open.

repatriation.claimant: Maria Altmann and the Bloch-Bauer heirs
repatriation.note: After Altmann v. Republic of Austria reached the US Supreme Court in 2004, an Austrian arbitration panel awarded the painting to the heirs in January 2006. It was sold to Ronald Lauder for the Neue Galerie for a reported $135M — then the highest price paid for a painting.

journey.event: Painted on commission from Ferdinand Bloch-Bauer
Bloch-Bauer assets seized after the Anschluss; the family flees
Restitution claim; litigation reaches the US Supreme Court in 2004
Arbitration panel awards the painting to the heirs

sources.title: Lost Art Database — Nazi-era spoliation register

identifyingRegisters.name: Lost Art Database
identifyingRegisters.requires: a loss connected to Nazi persecution, 1933–45

identifyingRegisters.name: Getty Provenance Index
identifyingRegisters.requires: passage through the European or American art market

identifyingRegisters.name: Art Loss Register
identifyingRegisters.requires: a registered loss, usually by an owner or insurer

weakRegisters.name: Wikidata
weakRegisters.why: Coverage follows scholarly and press attention rather than rule, so its silence carries little information.

blindRegisters.name: INTERPOL Stolen Works of Art
blindRegisters.why: Holds market-theft, archaeological losses; requires a theft reported to and recorded by a member-country police force.

blindRegisters.name: INTERPOL ID-Art
blindRegisters.why: Holds market-theft, archaeological losses; requires the same police report as the Stolen Works of Art database.

blindRegisters.name: FBI National Stolen Art File
blindRegisters.why: Holds market-theft losses; requires a law-enforcement submission and a value threshold.

blindRegisters.name: Carabinieri TPC
blindRegisters.why: Holds market-theft, archaeological losses; requires an Italian nexus — the archive is national.

blindRegisters.name: ICOM Red Lists
blindRegisters.why: Holds archaeological, colonial losses; requires membership of an at-risk object category from a listed region.

coverage.note: 3 of 9 registers could systematically name this object. Silence here is comparatively informative: records would be expected to exist.
coverage.comparability: This score is comparable only to other well covered objects. Comparison against a structurally uncovered object will understate how little was checked there.
